verses.2: “Listen carefully to my speech, And let this be your way of consolation.
verses.3: “Bear with me that I may speak; Then after I have spoken, you may mock me.
verses.4: “As for me, is my complaint to a mortal? Or why should I not be impatient?
verses.5: “Look at me, and be astonished, And put your hand over your mouth.
verses.6: “Even when I remember, I am disturbed, And horror takes hold of my flesh.
verses.7: “Why do the wicked still live, Grow old, and also become very powerful?
verses.8: “Their descendants endure with them in their sight, And their offspring before their eyes,
verses.9: Their houses are safe from fear, And the rod of God is not on them.
verses.10: “His ox mates without fail; His cow calves and does not miscarry.
verses.11: “They send out their boys like the flock, And their children dance.
verses.12: “They sing with the tambourine and harp, And rejoice at the sound of the flute.
verses.13: “They spend their days in prosperity, And suddenly they go down to Sheol.
verses.14: “Yet they say to God, ‘Go away from us! We do not even desire the knowledge of Your ways.
verses.15: ‘Who is the Almighty, that we should serve Him, And what would we gain if we plead with Him?’
verses.16: “Behold, their prosperity is not in their hand; The advice of the wicked is far from me.
verses.17: “How often is the lamp of the wicked put out, Or does their disaster fall on them? Does God apportion destruction in His anger?
verses.18: “Are they as straw before the wind, And like chaff which the storm carries away?
verses.19: “ You say, ‘God saves up a person’s wrongdoing for his sons.’ Let God repay him so that he may know it.
verses.20: “Let his own eyes see his destruction, And let him drink of the wrath of the Almighty.
verses.21: “For what does he care about his household after him, When the number of his months is at an end?
verses.22: “Can anyone teach God knowledge, In that He judges those on high?
verses.23: “One dies in his full strength, Being wholly undisturbed and at ease;
verses.24: His sides are filled with fat, And the marrow of his bones is wet,
verses.25: While another dies with a bitter soul, Never even tasting anything good.
verses.26: “Together they lie down in the dust, And maggots cover them.
verses.27: “Behold, I know your thoughts, And the plots you devise against me.
verses.28: “For you say, ‘Where is the house of the nobleman, And where is the tent, the dwelling places of the wicked?’
verses.29: “Have you not asked travelers, And do you not examine their evidence?
verses.30: “For the wicked person is spared a day of disaster; They are led away from a day of fury.
verses.31: “Who confronts him with his actions, And who repays him for what he has done?
verses.32: “When he is carried to the grave, People will keep watch over his tomb.
verses.33: “The clods of the valley will gently cover him; Moreover, all mankind will follow after him, While countless others go before him.
verses.34: “So how dare you give me empty comfort? For your answers remain nothing but falsehood!”
